cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. Under certain scenarios, Grappler component of TensorFlow can trigger a null pointer dereference. There are 2 places where this can occur, for the same malicious alteration of a `SavedModel` file (fixing the first one would trigger the same dereference in the second place). First, during constant folding, the `GraphDef` might not have the required nodes for the binary operation. If a node is missing, the correposning `mul_*child` would be null, and the dereference in the subsequent line would be incorrect. We have a similar issue during `IsIdentityConsumingSwitch`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. When a user does not supply arguments that determine a valid sparse tensor, `tf.raw_ops.SparseTensorSliceDataset` implementation can be made to dereference a null pointer. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/8d72537c6abf5a44103b57b9c2e22c14f5f49698/tensorflow/core/kernels/data/sparse_tensor_slice_dataset_op.cc#L240-L251) has some argument validation but fails to consider the case when either `indices` or `values` are provided for an empty sparse tensor when the other is not. If `indices` is empty, then [code that performs validation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/8d72537c6abf5a44103b57b9c2e22c14f5f49698/tensorflow/core/kernels/data/sparse_tensor_slice_dataset_op.cc#L260-L261) (i.e., checking that the indices are monotonically increasing) results in a null pointer dereference. If `indices` as provided by the user is empty, then `indices` in the C++ code above is backed by an empty `std::vector`, hence calling `indices->dim_size(0)` results in null pointer dereferencing (same as calling `std::vector::at()` on an empty vector). We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 02cc160e29d20631de3859c6653184e3f876b9d7. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

The IsMatrix check only verifies rank==2, so a shape like [N, 0] (N>0) passes validation, but the loop then reads indices->matrix<int64>()(i, 0) which accesses a non-existent column.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash or potential information disclosure of adjacent heap memory. An attacker who can control the shape of the indices tensor can trigger an out-of-bounds read.

The fix adds a check ensuring that if one is empty, the other must be too. The finding describes a different scenario: an out-of-bounds read from a [N,0]-shaped indices tensor accessing column 0. While this might be a separate valid concern, it is not the vulnerability addressed by this CVE and its fix.

actual_issue_summary: Null pointer dereference in SparseTensorSliceDataset when indices are empty but values are not (or vice versa). When indices is empty, the loop calling indices->dim_size(0) and subsequent SparseTensor::Create can dereference a null pointer. The fix adds validation that if either indices or values are empty, the other must also be empty.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library. In version 2.11.1 and prior, if incoming RTCP XR message contain block, the data field is not checked against the received packet size, potentially resulting in an out-of-bound read access. This affects all users that use PJMEDIA and RTCP XR. A malicious actor can send a RTCP XR message with an invalid packet size.

An attacker sends a crafted RTCP XR packet with a report block whose bt field indicates a large structure (e.g., BT_STATS or BT_VOIP_METRICS) but whose length field is small (e.g., 1). The code then reads structure fields far beyond the actual packet data.

actual_issue_summary: In pjmedia_rtcp_xr_rx_rtcp_xr(), when parsing RTCP XR report blocks, the code assigns report block pointers (rb_rr_time, rb_dlrr, rb_stats, rb_voip_mtc) without checking that the block's data actually fits within the received packet size. The fix adds bounds checks comparing (rb_hdr + sizeof(structure)) against (pkt + size) before each assignment, preventing out-of-bounds reads.

cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability was discovered in the PCRE2 library in the compile_xclass_matchingpath() function of the pcre2_jit_compile.c file. This involves a unicode property matching issue in JIT-compiled regular expressions. The issue occurs because the character was not fully read in case-less matching within JIT.

undesired_operation.description: In the PT_CLIST case when is_powerof2(other_cases[2] ^ other_cases[1]) is true and charoffset != 0, the generated JIT code uses the wrong XOR mask (other_cases[1] ^ other_cases[0] instead of other_cases[2] ^ other_cases[1]), producing incorrect character class matching code that can match wrong characters or fail to match correct ones.
code_snippets: OP2(SLJIT_OR, TMP2, 0, TMP2, 0, SLJIT_IMM, other_cases[1] ^ other_cases[0]);
undesired_operation.impact: If PCRE2 regex is used for security-sensitive input validation or filtering, the incorrectly compiled character class can cause the regex to match inputs it should reject or reject inputs it should accept, allowing security filter bypass.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2022-1586 fix changes `*cc == XCL_PROP` to `cc[-1] == XCL_PROP` in the condition `if (*cc == PT_CLIST && *cc == XCL_PROP)`. The bug was that `*cc` was compared to both PT_CLIST and XCL_PROP simultaneously, which (if they have different values) makes the condition always false, causing incorrect min/max range computation for caseless matching and leading to an out-of-bounds read. The finding instead describes a separate potential issue with XOR mask values in the PT_CLIST code generation path, which is unrelated to the actual vulnerability fixed.

actual_issue_summary: The condition `*cc == PT_CLIST && *cc == XCL_PROP` incorrectly checks `*cc` against XCL_PROP instead of `cc[-1]`. Since `*cc` was already confirmed to be PT_CLIST, and PT_CLIST != XCL_PROP, the condition was always false. This meant the caseless set min/max range was never properly computed for PT_CLIST properties, causing the JIT to use incorrect (overly broad) min/max values, leading to an out-of-bounds read when the character was not fully read in caseless matching.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation of SVDF in TFLite is [vulnerable to a null pointer error](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/svdf.cc#L300-L313). The [`GetVariableInput` function](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/kernel_util.cc#L115-L119) can return a null pointer but `GetTensorData` assumes that the argument is always a valid tensor. Furthermore, because `GetVariableInput` calls [`GetMutableInput`](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/kernel_util.cc#L82-L90) which might return `nullptr`, the `tensor->is_variable` expression can also trigger a null pointer exception. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 5b048e87e4e55990dae6b547add4dae59f4e1c76. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in MKL implementation of requantization, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantization_range_per_channel_op.cc) does not validate the dimensions of the `input` tensor. A similar issue occurs in `MklRequantizePerChannelOp`. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantize_per_channel_op.cc) does not perform full validation for all the input arguments. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e62869465573cb2d9b5053f1fa02a81fce21d69 and in the Github commit 203214568f5bc237603dbab6e1fd389f1572f5c9. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2`, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/84d053187cb80d975ef2b9684d4b61981bca0c41/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_op.cc#L59) has some validation but does not check that `min_range` and `max_range` both have the same non-zero number of elements. If `axis` is provided (i.e., not `-1`), then validation should check that it is a value in range for the rank of `input` tensor and then the lengths of `min_range` and `max_range` inputs match the `axis` dimension of the `input` tensor. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 6da6620efad397c85493b8f8667b821403516708. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

--- vulnerable
+++ fixed
@@ -5,7 +5,50 @@
 
     int num_slices = 1;
     if (axis_ > -1) {
+      OP_REQUIRES(
+          ctx, input.dims() > axis_,
+          errors::InvalidArgument(
+              "Axis is on a zero-based index, so its value must always be less "
+              "than number of input's dims, but given axis value was ",
+              axis_, " and input's dims was ", input.dims()));
       num_slices = input.dim_size(axis_);
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_min_range.dims() == 1,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is specified, min_range must be a 1-D tensor "
+                      "whose size matches the axis dimension of the input and "
+                      "output tensors, but min_range dims are ",
+                      input_min_range.dims()));
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_min_range.dim_size(0) == num_slices,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is specified, min_range must be a 1-D tensor "
+                      "whose size matches the axis dimension of the input and "
+                      "output tensors, but min_range is a 1-D tensor of size ",
+                      input_min_range.dim_size(0),
+                      " and input's axis dimension is of size ", num_slices));
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_max_range.dims() == 1,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is specified, max_range must be a 1-D tensor "
+                      "whose size matches the axis dimension of the input and "
+                      "output tensors, but max_range dims are ",
+                      input_max_range.dims()));
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_max_range.dim_size(0) == num_slices,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is specified, max_range must be a 1-D tensor "
+                      "whose size matches the axis dimension of the input and "
+                      "output tensors, but max_range is a 1-D tensor of size ",
+                      input_max_range.dim_size(0),
+                      " and input's axis dimension is of size ", num_slices));
+    } else {
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_min_range.NumElements() == 1,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is not specified, min_range must contain a "
+                      "single float element, but it contains ",
+                      input_min_range.NumElements(), " elements"));
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_max_range.NumElements() == 1,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is not specified, max_range must contain a "

actual_issue_summary: In tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2, incomplete validation allows: (1) axis_ values >= input.dims() causing out-of-bounds access on dimension sizes, (2) min_range/max_range tensors with mismatched sizes relative to the axis dimension causing out-of-bounds reads in the quantization loop, and (3) empty min_range/max_range tensors when axis=-1 causing null pointer dereference. cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation of sparse reduction operations in TensorFlow can trigger accesses outside of bounds of heap allocated data. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/a1bc56203f21a5a4995311825ffaba7a670d7747/tensorflow/core/kernels/sparse_reduce_op.cc#L217-L228) fails to validate that each reduction group does not overflow and that each corresponding index does not point to outside the bounds of the input tensor. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 87158f43f05f2720a374f3e6d22a7aaa3a33f750. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

If sparse tensor indices contain values outside the valid range of the declared shape, the computed idx can be negative or exceed the output tensor size, causing an out-of-bounds write.
undesired_operation.impact: An attacker can craft sparse tensor indices that produce an out-of-bounds index into the output tensor, leading to heap corruption (arbitrary write), denial of service via crash, or potentially arbitrary code execution.

The finding correctly identifies the missing bounds check on idx before the write to out_flat, which is the core of this CVE.

This allows heap out-of-bounds access via crafted sparse tensor indices. The fix adds OP_REQUIRES checks for both conditions before the write.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C. A buffer overflow vulnerability in versions 2.12 and prior affects applications that use PJSIP DNS resolution. It doesn't affect PJSIP users who utilize an external resolver. This vulnerability is related to CVE-2023-27585. The difference is that this issue is in parsing the query record `parse_rr()`, while the issue in CVE-2023-27585 is in `parse_query()`. A patch is available in the `master` branch of the `pjsip/pjproject` GitHub repository. A workaround is to disable DNS resolution in PJSIP config (by setting `nameserver_count` to zero) or use an external resolver instead.

After parsing the name field, the code reads 4 bytes (type + dnsclass) without verifying that the remaining buffer is large enough, allowing out-of-bounds memory access from a crafted DNS response. cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation for `tf.raw_ops.BoostedTreesCreateEnsemble` can result in a use after free error if an attacker supplies specially crafted arguments. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/f24faa153ad31a4b51578f8181d3aaab77a1ddeb/tensorflow/core/kernels/boosted_trees/resource_ops.cc#L55) uses a reference counted resource and decrements the refcount if the initialization fails, as it should. However, when the code was written, the resource was represented as a naked pointer but later refactoring has changed it to be a smart pointer. Thus, when the pointer leaves the scope, a subsequent `free`-ing of the resource occurs, but this fails to take into account that the refcount has already reached 0, thus the resource has been already freed. During this double-free process, members of the resource object are accessed for cleanup but they are invalid as the entire resource has been freed. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 5ecec9c6fbdbc6be03295685190a45e7eee726ab. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash, or potentially arbitrary code execution through heap corruption caused by a double-free.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core vulnerability: after Unref() frees the resource, the unique_ptr still owns the pointer and will attempt to delete it again when it goes out of scope, causing a double-free (which is closely related to use-after-free, CWE-416). The fix adds result.release() after Unref() to prevent the unique_ptr from freeing the already-freed object. CWE-415 (double-free) is essentially the same root cause as the CVE's CWE-416 (use-after-free) in this context.

actual_issue_summary: In BoostedTreesCreateEnsemble, when InitFromSerialized fails, result->Unref() decrements the refcount to 0 and frees the resource, but the unique_ptr 'result' still owns the pointer. When the unique_ptr destructor runs, it attempts to delete the already-freed object, causing a use-after-free/double-free. The fix adds result.release() after Unref() to relinquish unique_ptr ownership before the pointer becomes dangling.

cve_desc: common/password.c in Pengutronix barebox through 2021.07.0 leaks timing information because strncmp is used during hash comparison.

undesired_operation.description: strncmp is used to compare binary hash digests. strncmp stops at the first null byte ('\0'), so if either the computed hash or the stored hash contains a null byte before position hash_len/keylen, the comparison is truncated. An attacker can find a password whose hash shares only the bytes before the first null byte with the stored hash, bypassing authentication.
undesired_operation.impact: Authentication bypass — if the stored password hash contains a null byte at position N (0 <= N < hash_len), the attacker only needs to find a password whose hash matches the first N bytes, drastically reducing the search space (e.g., a null byte at position 0 means any password whose hash also starts with 0x00 passes).

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2021-37848) is specifically about timing side-channel information leakage because strncmp is used for hash comparison, allowing an attacker to infer hash contents by measuring comparison time. The finding instead describes a null-byte truncation issue (strncmp stopping at '\0' in binary data), which is a different vulnerability class (CWE-187/CWE-305 vs CWE-200/CWE-208). While both issues stem from the same root cause (using strncmp instead of a constant-time memory comparison), the finding does not identify or describe the timing leak that is the actual CVE.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerability is a timing side-channel in password hash comparison. strncmp was used to compare password hashes, which is not constant-time — it returns early on the first differing byte. An attacker could measure response times to infer information about the stored password hash byte-by-byte. The fix replaces strncmp with crypto_memneq, which performs constant-time comparison.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2015-3885 is an integer overflow in ljpeg_start() where the `len` variable is declared as `int`. When (data[2]<<8|data[3]) is 0 or 1, `len` becomes negative (-2 or -1). When passed to fread() as a size_t parameter, this negative value becomes a very large positive number, causing a buffer overflow of the stack-allocated `data[0x10000]` array. The fix changes `len` from `int` to `ushort`, ensuring it wraps to a large unsigned short value rather than becoming negative, and naturally stays within bounds of the buffer.

This enables arbitrary out-of-bounds memory read/write through subsequent PostScript string operations, potentially leading to remote code execution.

The subsequent loop then copies `len` elements past the end of the allocated stack buffer.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2022-0890 is a NULL pointer dereference (CWE-703/CWE-476) that occurs when fibers handle 15+ arguments. The fix packs 15+ arguments into an array to prevent the issue. The finding describes a hypothetical integer overflow in len+2 leading to heap buffer overflow, which is a different class of vulnerability entirely. In practice, `len` comes from the number of actual Ruby arguments and wouldn't reach MRB_INT_MAX. The real bug is about how large argument counts (>=15) are stored as `cibase->n` without being packed into an array, leading to a NULL pointer dereference when the VM later tries to access them.

However, the VM expects 15+ arguments to be packed into an array (n=15 is a sentinel value). This mismatch caused a NULL pointer dereference when the VM tried to process the arguments. The fix packs 15+ arguments into an array and sets len=15 as the sentinel. Additionally, the ci-- for vmexec was moved before the stack operations.

--- vulnerable

undesired_operation.description: When bufIsChanged is true and bt_terminal is also true (and bt_help, w_p_pvw, and b_p_ro are all false), a space character overwrites the NUL terminator of the string in NameBuff, but no subsequent vim_snprintf call re-establishes null termination. The unterminated string is then passed to screen_puts and mb_string2cells which read until NUL, causing an out-of-bounds read past the intended string boundary.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read of heap memory beyond the NameBuff buffer. Could lead to information disclosure (heap contents rendered to screen) or a crash if memory is unmapped.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause: the missing length check before `*(p + len++) = ' '` which can write past the end of NameBuff when len equals MAXPATHL-1. This is exactly the bug fixed by adding `&& len < MAXPATHL - 1`. The finding frames it as an OOB read consequence (the space overwrites the NUL terminator leading to unterminated string reads), while the CVE is classified as heap-based buffer overflow (the write itself goes past the buffer). Both describe the same underlying vulnerability — the missing bounds check on the same line of code. The specific scenario described (bufIsChanged true, bt_terminal true) is one valid path where the space is written but no subsequent snprintf re-adds a NUL, making the consequences clearer.

actual_issue_summary: In win_redr_status(), when appending a space character to NameBuff via `*(p + len++) = ' '`, there was no check that len < MAXPATHL - 1. If the buffer name filled NameBuff to its maximum size (MAXPATHL), the space write would go one byte past the end of the buffer, causing a heap-based buffer overflow. The fix adds `&& len < MAXPATHL - 1` to the condition guarding the space append.

cve_desc: An issue was discovered in disable_priv_mode in shell.c in GNU Bash through 5.0 patch 11. By default, if Bash is run with its effective UID not equal to its real UID, it will drop privileges by setting its effective UID to its real UID. However, it does so incorrectly. On Linux and other systems that support "saved UID" functionality, the saved UID is not dropped. An attacker with command execution in the shell can use "enable -f" for runtime loading of a new builtin, which can be a shared object that calls setuid() and therefore regains privileges. However, binaries running with an effective UID of 0 are unaffected.

undesired_operation.description: If setuid() fails (for any reason other than EAGAIN when EXIT_ON_SETUID_FAILURE is defined), execution continues and the bookkeeping variables current_user.euid/egid are updated as though privilege was successfully dropped, while the process actually retains its original elevated effective uid.
undesired_operation.impact: The program believes it is running unprivileged (current_user.euid == current_user.uid) but actually retains elevated privileges. Subsequent code that trusts this bookkeeping will allow privileged operations that should have been denied, potentially leading to privilege escalation.

step_by_step_execution.description: setuid(current_user.uid) is called and fails

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: setuid failed

step_by_step_execution.description: Error is logged but execution continues past the if block
Bookkeeping is unconditionally updated

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2019-18276 is about setuid() succeeding but not dropping the saved UID on Linux systems that support saved UIDs. The fix replaces setuid() with setresuid() to explicitly set real, effective, AND saved UIDs. The finding describes a different scenario: setuid() failing and bookkeeping being wrong. The real vulnerability occurs when setuid() succeeds — it drops the effective UID but leaves the saved UID intact, allowing re-escalation via setuid() later. This finding identifies a different (secondary) issue, not the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2019-18276: disable_priv_mode() uses setuid()/setgid() which drop the effective UID/GID but do not drop the saved UID/GID on systems supporting saved IDs. An attacker can call setuid() to regain the original privileged UID. The fix uses setresuid()/setresgid() to explicitly set real, effective, and saved IDs all at once.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C language implementing standard based protocols such as SIP, SDP, RTP, STUN, TURN, and ICE. In affected versions if the incoming STUN message contains an ERROR-CODE attribute, the header length is not checked before performing a subtraction operation, potentially resulting in an integer underflow scenario. This issue affects all users that use STUN. A malicious actor located within the victim’s network may forge and send a specially crafted UDP (STUN) message that could remotely execute arbitrary code on the victim’s machine. Users are advised to upgrade as soon as possible. There are no known workarounds.

undesired_operation.description: When attr->hdr.length (read from attacker-controlled buffer) is less than 4, the subtraction `attr->hdr.length - 4` produces a negative value assigned to value.slen. When pj_strdup then uses this negative slen (interpreted as a very large unsigned size_t for allocation/memcpy), it causes a massive out-of-bounds read and/or heap overflow.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash, potential information disclosure or code execution. An attacker sends a crafted STUN message with an ERROR-CODE attribute whose length field is less than 4, causing an integer underflow that leads to a massive out-of-bounds memory read/copy.

initial_state.value: pointer to attacker-controlled STUN message buffer

value.slen is computed as attr->hdr.length - 4
pj_strdup is called with negative value.slen, which when cast to size_t becomes a very large value, causing out-of-bounds read/heap overflow

out_state.value: -2 interpreted as ~4294967294 when used as size_t

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies the CVE-2021-37706 vulnerability: the missing check on attr->hdr.length before subtracting 4 leads to an integer underflow in value.slen, which is exactly what the fix addresses by clamping value.slen to 0 when negative.

actual_issue_summary: In decode_errcode_attr(), when a STUN ERROR-CODE attribute has a header length less than 4, the subtraction `attr->hdr.length - 4` produces a negative value for value.slen. This negative length is then passed to pj_strdup, where it gets interpreted as a very large unsigned value, leading to out-of-bounds memory operations. The fix adds a check to clamp value.slen to 0 if it becomes negative.

If those callbacks reach the success path (past the connect_finish check), they dereference data without a NULL check, causing a crash. When those attempts' callbacks fired and reached code paths that dereferenced data without NULL checks, it caused crashes. The fix removes the data=NULL pattern and instead uses cancellation-based checking (task_completed_or_cancelled) to properly handle completed/cancelled tasks, ensuring the parent GTask remains alive during enumeration.

cve_desc: LibVNC before commit 7b1ef0ffc4815cab9a96c7278394152bdc89dc4d contains heap out-of-bound write vulnerability inside structure in VNC client code that can result remote code execution

The finding's CWE-190 (integer overflow) matches the root cause, and the out-of-bounds access consequence is also correct. cve_desc: Integer overflow in the sctp_setsockopt_auth_key function in net/sctp/socket.c in the Stream Control Transmission Protocol (sctp) implementation in the Linux kernel 2.6.24-rc1 through 2.6.26.3 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (panic) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted sca_keylength field associated with the SCTP_AUTH_KEY option.

cve_desc: There is an assertion failure in SingleComponentLSScan::ParseMCU in singlecomponentlsscan.cpp in libjpeg before 1.64 via an empty JPEG-LS scan.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation for `tf.raw_ops.FractionalAvgPoolGrad` can be tricked into accessing data outside of bounds of heap allocated buffers. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/f24faa153ad31a4b51578f8181d3aaab77a1ddeb/tensorflow/core/kernels/fractional_avg_pool_op.cc#L205) does not validate that the input tensor is non-empty. Thus, code constructs an empty `EigenDoubleMatrixMap` and then accesses this buffer with indices that are outside of the empty area. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 0f931751fb20f565c4e94aa6df58d54a003cdb30. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: No validation that out_batch <= in_batch. The outer loop iterates b from 0 to out_batch-1, computing in_index = (b * in_rows + in_r) * in_cols + in_c. When out_batch > in_batch, in_index exceeds the allocated in_backprop_tensor_temp_mat column count (in_cols * in_rows * in_batch), causing an out-of-bounds heap write.
code_snippets: const int64_t in_index = (b * in_rows + in_r) * in_cols + in_c;
undesired_operation.impact: Heap out-of-bounds write leading to potential arbitrary code execution or denial of service

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2021-37651) is specifically about missing validation that input tensor dimensions (in_batch, in_rows, in_cols, in_depth) are non-zero, which causes an empty EigenDoubleMatrixMap to be created and then accessed out of bounds. The fix adds checks that these four dimensions are != 0. The finding describes a different issue about out_batch > in_batch leading to OOB access, which is not what was fixed and not what the CVE describes.

actual_issue_summary: Missing validation that the original input tensor shape dimensions (in_batch, in_rows, in_cols, in_depth) are non-zero in FractionalAvgPoolGrad. When any dimension is 0, an empty EigenDoubleMatrixMap is constructed and subsequently accessed with out-of-bounds indices, leading to heap buffer access violations.

The fix adds a 1MB limit on the payload size, rejecting connections that exceed it.

actual_issue_summary: When an XM module file has an instrument header with size less than 4 (in the v0x104 path), the expression `instr[y].size - 4` underflows to a huge unsigned value, causing f.read() to write far beyond a 29-byte stack buffer. The fix adds a `>= 4` check before entering the branch that computes `instr[y].size - 4`.

cve_desc: An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel through 5.16-rc6. There is a lack of check after calling vzalloc() and lack of free after allocation in drivers/media/test-drivers/vidtv/vidtv_s302m.c.

undesired_operation.description: vzalloc for e->encoder_buf can fail and return NULL, but the return value is never checked. The function returns a seemingly valid encoder object (non-NULL 'e') with e->encoder_buf set to NULL. Any subsequent use of e->encoder_buf will dereference a NULL pointer.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via kernel NULL pointer dereference when the encoder buffer is later accessed by the encode function.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: kzalloc succeeded, e is non-NULL

No check is performed on e->encoder_buf; execution continues and eventually returns e with a NULL encoder_buf

judgments.reasoning: This directly matches the CVE description: 'lack of check after calling vzalloc()'. The fix adds exactly this check. CWE-476 matches the assigned CWE.

undesired_operation.description: When kzalloc for ctx fails, the error path calls kfree(e) but does not free e->name (allocated by kstrdup) or e->encoder_buf (allocated by vzalloc), causing memory leaks.
undesired_operation.impact: Kernel memory leak. Repeated triggering can exhaust kernel memory, leading to denial of service.

e->name is allocated via kstrdup
e->encoder_buf is allocated via vzalloc

actual_issue_summary: vzalloc() for e->encoder_buf is not checked for NULL return, leading to potential NULL pointer dereference. Additionally, the error path when ctx allocation fails does not free e->name or e->encoder_buf, causing memory leaks. The fix adds a NULL check after vzalloc and properly frees all allocated resources in error paths.

cve_desc: A code execution vulnerability exists in the DL_Dxf::handleLWPolylineData functionality of Ribbonsoft dxflib 3.17.0. A specially-crafted .dxf file can lead to a heap buffer overflow. An attacker can provide a malicious file to trigger this vulnerability.

undesired_operation.description: When groupCode 42 is received before any groupCode 10, vertexIndex is -1, causing an out-of-bounds write at vertices[4*(-1)+3] = vertices[-1].
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds heap write (writing 8 bytes before the vertices buffer). Could corrupt heap metadata or adjacent objects, potentially leading to code execution.

groupCode is 42

groupCode is 42 which is > 30

out_state.value: toReal(groupValue) — attacker-controlled double written before buffer start

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the missing vertexIndex>=0 check in the groupCode==42 branch, which is exactly the vulnerability fixed in the commit. When vertexIndex is -1 and groupCode is 42, the code writes before the heap buffer, causing a heap buffer overflow (CWE-787 is consistent with CVE-2021-21897's heap buffer overflow).

actual_issue_summary: In handleLWPolylineData, when groupCode==42 is processed, there was no check that vertexIndex>=0. Since vertexIndex starts at -1 and is only incremented on groupCode==10, a crafted DXF file sending groupCode 42 before any groupCode 10 causes an out-of-bounds write at a negative index into the heap-allocated vertices array.

cve_desc: AutoTrace v0.40.0 was discovered to contain a heap overflow via the ReadImage function at input-bmp.c:660.

undesired_operation.impact: Stack-based buffer overflow allows arbitrary code execution. An attacker crafts a BMP file with biSize set to 108 or 124, causing 104 or 120 bytes to be written into a 64-byte stack buffer, overwriting the return address and other stack data.

First 12 bytes of file header are read into buffer, then biSize (4 bytes) is read

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause of CVE-2022-32323: when biSize is 108 or 124, ReadOK writes 104 or 120 bytes into a 64-byte stack buffer, causing a buffer overflow. The fix increases the buffer to 128 bytes to accommodate these larger headers. The CVE description mentions 'heap overflow' but the actual issue is a stack buffer overflow, and the finding accurately describes the vulnerability fixed by the commit.

actual_issue_summary: The stack buffer 'buffer[64]' is too small to hold BMP Version 4 (108-byte) or Version 5 (124-byte) headers. When biSize is 108 or 124, ReadOK writes biSize-4 (104 or 120) bytes into the 64-byte buffer, causing a buffer overflow. The fix increases the buffer size from 64 to 128 bytes.

cve_desc: The ip6gre_err function in net/ipv6/ip6_gre.c in the Linux kernel allows remote attackers to have unspecified impact via vectors involving GRE flags in an IPv6 packet, which trigger an out-of-bounds access.

undesired_operation.description: The GRE key is read from an out-of-bounds memory location because `grehlen` includes `offset` but is used as an index relative to pointer `p` which already starts at `skb->data + offset`, double-counting the offset.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read in kernel context. Can cause a kernel crash (denial of service) or potentially leak kernel heap/slab memory contents via side channels. An attacker can trigger this by sending a crafted ICMPv6 error message referencing a GRE-over-IPv6 packet with the GRE_KEY flag set.

state.value: offset + 4 + optional_fields (includes IPv6 header size via offset, e.g. 44 or 48)
(__be16 *)(skb->data + offset), already past the IPv6 header

initial_state.value: passed by caller, typically 40 (size of IPv6 header)

step_by_step_execution.description: grehlen is initialized to offset + 4, e.g. 44 when offset=40
flags is read from p[0], the first 2 bytes of the GRE header

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker crafts packet without VERSION or ROUTING flags to avoid early return

relevant_state.value: GRE_KEY set, VERSION and ROUTING clear

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: GRE_KEY flag is set in the crafted packet

step_by_step_execution.description: grehlen increased by 4 for GRE_KEY; GRE_CSUM not set so no further increase

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: pskb_may_pull checks 48 bytes from skb->data, which is enough for the IPv6 header + basic GRE + key field. The packet has sufficient data.

step_by_step_execution.description: The key extraction expression computes ((__be32 *)p) + (grehlen / 4) - 1 = p + 48/4 - 1 = p + 11. Since p = skb->data + 40, this reads at skb->data + 40 + 44 = skb->data + 84. The actual GRE key should be at skb->data + 44 (offset 40 + 4 byte base header). This is ~40 bytes beyond the intended location.

out_state.value: skb->data + 84 (out of bounds, should be skb->data + 44)

judgments.reasoning: The finding precisely identifies the CVE-2017-5897 vulnerability: grehlen includes the offset (IPv6 header size) but is used relative to pointer p which already accounts for the offset, causing the GRE key to be read ~40 bytes past its actual location. This is exactly the out-of-bounds read fixed by the commit.

actual_issue_summary: In ip6gre_err(), grehlen was initialized to `offset + 4` (including the IPv6 header offset), but then used in the expression `*(((__be32 *)p) + (grehlen / 4) - 1)` where `p` already points to `skb->data + offset`. This double-counting of the offset caused the GRE key to be read ~40 bytes beyond its actual position, resulting in an out-of-bounds read. The fix changes grehlen to track only the GRE header length and uses a separate key_off variable to correctly locate the key field.

cve_desc: Rizin is a UNIX-like reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset. In versions up to and including 0.3.1 there is a heap-based out of bounds write in parse_die() when reversing an AMD64 ELF binary with DWARF debug info. When a malicious AMD64 ELF binary is opened by a victim user, Rizin may crash or execute unintended actions. No workaround are known and users are advised to upgrade.

undesired_operation.description: When abbrev->count is 0, the subtraction `abbrev->count - 1` wraps around to SIZE_MAX (since size_t is unsigned), causing the loop to iterate ~2^64 times and access die->attr_values[i] and abbrev->defs[i] massively out of bounds.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap/stack buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash when processing a crafted DWARF binary with an abbreviation declaration that has count == 0.

step_by_step_execution.description: The loop condition evaluates abbrev->count - 1. Since abbrev->count is 0 and size_t is unsigned, this wraps to SIZE_MAX.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: i is 0, abbrev->count - 1 is SIZE_MAX, so 0 < SIZE_MAX is true and the loop body executes.

step_by_step_execution.description: memset and parse_attr_value write to die->attr_values[i] and read from abbrev->defs[i] out of bounds as i increases beyond the allocated sizes.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-43814: when abbrev->count is 0, the unsigned subtraction wraps to SIZE_MAX causing an out-of-bounds heap write in parse_die(). The fix adds a guard `if (abbrev->count)` before the loop, which is precisely what the commit does.

actual_issue_summary: When abbrev->count is 0, the expression `abbrev->count - 1` in the for-loop condition undergoes unsigned integer wraparound to SIZE_MAX, causing massive out-of-bounds heap writes via die->attr_values[i] and reads via abbrev->defs[i]. The fix adds a check `if (abbrev->count)` before entering the loop.

cve_desc: In Singular before 4.3.1, a predictable /tmp pathname is used (e.g., by sdb.cc), which allows local users to gain the privileges of other users via a procedure in a file under /tmp. NOTE: this CVE Record is about sdb.cc and similar files in the Singular interface that have predictable /tmp pathnames; this CVE Record is not about the lack of a safe temporary-file creation capability in the Singular language.

undesired_operation.description: sprintf writes the PID as a decimal string into the filename buffer starting at offset 7, but the buffer was allocated as a duplicate of "/tmp/sd000000" (14 bytes total). A PID with 7 or more digits (>= 1000000) overflows the heap buffer, since only 6 character positions (indices 7-12) plus a null terminator (index 13) are available.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow: a PID of 7+ digits writes past the end of the 14-byte heap allocation, corrupting heap metadata or adjacent objects, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution.

step_by_step_execution.description: filename is allocated as a duplicate of "/tmp/sd000000" (14 bytes including null terminator)
sprintf writes PID decimal string starting at filename+7

out_state.value: write of 7+ chars starting at index 7 overflows the 14-byte buffer

judgments.reasoning: The CVE is about using a predictable /tmp pathname (based on PID) that allows local privilege escalation via symlink attacks (CWE-269). The fix replaces the PID-based predictable filename with mkstemp() for secure temporary file creation. The finding describes a theoretical heap buffer overflow with very large PIDs, which is a different issue entirely and not what CVE-2022-40299 is about.

actual_issue_summary: The function used a predictable temporary file path based on PID (/tmp/sd<PID>) instead of a securely generated unique filename. This allowed local attackers to predict the filename and create symlinks to exploit a TOCTOU race condition, potentially gaining privileges of other users. The fix replaces the PID-based naming with mkstemp() which creates a unique, unpredictable temporary file atomically.

cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read in the BGP daemon of FRRouting FRR before 8.4 may lead to a segmentation fault and denial of service. This occurs in bgp_capability_msg_parse in bgpd/bgp_packet.c.

undesired_operation.description: memcpy copies sizeof(struct capability) bytes from pnt+1 into cap, but the prior bounds check only guarantees 3 bytes are available from pnt (i.e., 2 bytes from pnt+1). If sizeof(struct capability) > 2, this reads past the end of the input buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from attacker-controlled network packet data. Could cause a crash (denial of service) or leak adjacent heap/stack memory contents into the cap structure, which are then used for further processing.

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly identifies the CVE-2022-37032 vulnerability: the memcpy of sizeof(struct capability) from pnt+1 occurs before the capability length check, reading beyond the available buffer. The fix moves the memcpy after the length validation and reduces the copy size to just struct capability_mp_data. This matches the CVE description of an out-of-bounds read in bgp_capability_msg_parse.

actual_issue_summary: In bgp_capability_msg_parse, memcpy(&cap, pnt+1, sizeof(struct capability)) copies the entire (large) capability structure from the input buffer before validating the capability's length field. This causes an out-of-bounds read when the remaining buffer is smaller than sizeof(struct capability). The fix moves the memcpy after the length check and copies only the smaller capability_mp_data structure. Additionally, afi/safi values are now validated via bgp_afi_safi_valid_indices before being used as array indices.

cve_desc: A race condition exists in Eternal Terminal prior to version 6.2.0 that allows an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization socket, enabling the attacker to login to other systems as the targeted users. The bug is in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId().

undesired_operation.description: strcpy copies pipePath (attacker-controlled endpoint name) into fixed-size local.sun_path buffer without bounds checking, causing a stack buffer overflow if pipePath exceeds sizeof(local.sun_path) (typically 108 bytes).
undesired_operation.impact: Stack-based buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker who controls the endpoint name can overwrite the return address and other stack variables.

judgments.reasoning: CVE-2022-24950 is specifically about a race condition in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId() and acceptNewConnection() due to missing mutex locks, allowing an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization sockets. The finding describes a buffer overflow (strcpy→strncpy), which is a separate minor hardening fix included in the same commit but is not the vulnerability described by CVE-2022-24950.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2022-24950 is a race condition in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId() and acceptNewConnection(). These functions access the shared idInfoMap without holding a lock, allowing concurrent access that could let an authenticated attacker hijack another user's SSH authorization socket. The fix adds a recursive_mutex (routerMutex) and lock_guard calls to both functions. The strcpy→strncpy change in PipeSocketHandler is a separate hardening fix in the same commit.

cve_desc: A null pointer de-reference was found in the way samba kerberos server handled missing sname in TGS-REQ (Ticket Granting Server - Request). An authenticated user could use this flaw to crash the samba server.

undesired_operation.description: In the constrained delegation block, tp and tpn are overwritten without freeing the values previously allocated in the S4U2Self block, causing a memory leak.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service through memory exhaustion. An attacker sending repeated S4U2Proxy (S4U2Self + constrained delegation) requests can leak memory on each request, eventually exhausting KDC memory.

judgments.reasoning: The CVE-2021-3671 is a null pointer dereference caused by missing validation of sname (b->sname) in TGS-REQ before it is dereferenced with `*s`. The fix adds an `else if (s == NULL)` check. This finding describes a completely different issue — a potential memory leak in the constrained delegation path when tp/tpn are overwritten — which is unrelated to the null pointer dereference vulnerability.

actual_issue_summary: When b->sname is NULL in a TGS-REQ (and the enc_tkt_in_skey path is not taken), the pointer `s` remains NULL and is dereferenced as `*s` in the call to `_krb5_principalname2krb5_principal(context, &sp, *s, r)`, causing a null pointer dereference crash. The fix adds a check for `s == NULL` before that dereference.

cve_desc: JasPer 2.0.12 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer over-read and application crash) via a crafted image, related to the jp2_decode function in libjasper/jp2/jp2_dec.c.

cve_desc: TigerVNC version prior to 1.10.1 is vulnerable to stack use-after-return, which occurs due to incorrect usage of stack memory in ZRLEDecoder. If decoding routine would throw an exception, ZRLEDecoder may try to access stack variable, which has been already freed during the process of stack unwinding. Exploitation of this vulnerability could potentially result into remote code execution. This attack appear to be exploitable via network connectivity.

An attacker can supply an index >= palSize to read uninitialized stack memory from palette[], which is then written to the pixel buffer.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2019-15691 is about stack use-after-return in ZlibInStream. The fix changes removeUnderlying() to flushUnderlying() + setUnderlying(NULL, 0) to prevent accessing freed stack memory when an exception occurs during decoding. The finding describes a completely different issue (uninitialized palette read), which is unrelated to the CVE and the actual fix.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerability is a stack use-after-return in ZRLEDecoder's use of ZlibInStream. When an exception is thrown during decoding, ZlibInStream's destructor or later operations may access the stack-allocated InStream pointer (set via setUnderlying) that has already been freed during stack unwinding. The fix replaces removeUnderlying() with flushUnderlying() + setUnderlying(NULL, 0) to properly clean up the underlying stream reference, and the ZlibInStream class was also made more robust against accessing invalid memory.

cve_desc: There's a flaw in jasper's jpc encoder in versions prior to 2.0.23. Crafted input provided to jasper by an attacker could cause an arbitrary out-of-bounds write. This could potentially affect data confidentiality, integrity, or application availability.

The validation that maxrlvls >= 1 occurs AFTER the loop, and there is no upper-bound check before the loop, so a large maxrlvls value causes out-of-bounds writes to fixed-size arrays.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap or stack buffer overflow allowing arbitrary code execution or denial of service. An attacker controlling the encoding options string can write past the bounds of the prcwidthexpns/prcheightexpns arrays in the jpc_enc_tccp_t structure, corrupting adjacent memory.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2020-27828. The fix adds an upper-bound check (tccp->maxrlvls > JPC_MAXRLVLS) right after the OPT_MAXRLVLS assignment, preventing the out-of-bounds write in the loop that uses maxrlvls as its bound. The finding correctly identifies the missing validation and the resulting heap buffer overflow.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2020-27828 is caused by a missing upper-bound check on tccp->maxrlvls when set from user input via OPT_MAXRLVLS. The unchecked value is used as a loop bound to write into fixed-size arrays (prcwidthexpns/prcheightexpns), causing an out-of-bounds heap write. The fix adds a check that maxrlvls does not exceed JPC_MAXRLVLS.

cve_desc: A denial of service vulnerability was found in rsyslog in the imptcp module. An attacker could send a specially crafted message to the imptcp socket, which would cause rsyslog to crash. Versions before 8.27.0 are vulnerable.

undesired_operation.description: memcpy is called with octatesToCopy derived from a negative pThis->iOctetsRemain (due to integer overflow during octet count parsing). When a negative int is implicitly cast to size_t for memcpy's length parameter, it becomes a very large value, causing a massive heap buffer overflow.
undesired_operation.impact: Remote heap buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker can send a crafted TCP syslog message with an extremely long digit sequence as the octet count to trigger the overflow.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the integer overflow in iOctetsRemain during octet count parsing (CWE-190), which is exactly what CVE-2018-16881 describes. The fix adds a bound check (iOctetsRemain <= 200000000) to prevent the overflow. The finding also correctly traces the consequence: the overflowed value leads to dangerous behavior in memcpy, causing a crash (denial of service). This matches the CVE description of a DoS via specially crafted message to the imptcp socket.

actual_issue_summary: Integer overflow in rsyslog's imptcp module during octet-counted TCP framing. The iOctetsRemain field is computed as `iOctetsRemain * 10 + c - '0'` without any upper bound check, allowing an attacker to send a message with an extremely large octet count that overflows the integer. This leads to a crash (segmentation fault). The fix adds a check that iOctetsRemain <= 200000000 before multiplying, and falls back to octet stuffing framing if the value is too large.

cve_desc: Integer signedness error in the simplestring_addn function in simplestring.c in xmlrpc-epi through 0.54.2, as used in PHP before 5.5.38, 5.6.x before 5.6.24, and 7.x before 7.0.9, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer overflow) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a long first argument to the PHP xmlrpc_encode_request function.

cve_desc: A heap buffer overflow issue was found in ImageMagick. When an application processes a malformed TIFF file, it could lead to undefined behavior or a crash causing a denial of service.

undesired_operation.description: Integer overflow in extent calculation for tile buffer allocation. Similar to the strip case, `4*(samples_per_pixel+1)*MagickMax(rows*TIFFTileRowSize(tiff), TIFFTileSize(tiff))` can overflow 32-bit arithmetic when tsize_t is 32-bit, resulting in a small buffer allocation followed by TIFFReadTile writing more data than the buffer can hold.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow allowing potential arbitrary code execution via crafted TIFF with large tiles and multiple samples per pixel.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE fix is in the ReadStripMethod case, changing `extent=4*(samples_per_pixel+1)*TIFFStripSize(tiff)` to `extent=4*((image->depth+7)/8)*(samples_per_pixel+1)*TIFFStripSize(tiff)`. The buffer was undersized because it didn't account for image depth. The finding instead points to the ReadTileMethod's extent calculation, which is a different code path that was NOT changed by the fix. The finding identifies the wrong location.

actual_issue_summary: In the ReadStripMethod case of ReadTIFFImage, the strip_pixels buffer extent was calculated as `4*(samples_per_pixel+1)*TIFFStripSize(tiff)`, which did not account for `image->depth`. The fix multiplies by `(image->depth+7)/8` to ensure the buffer is large enough, preventing a heap buffer overflow when processing TIFF files with higher bit depths.

cve_desc: MariaDB Server v10.6 and below was discovered to contain an use-after-free in the component my_strcasecmp_8bit, which is exploited via specially crafted SQL statements.

undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read leading to information disclosure or crash (denial of service). An attacker providing a very short plugin name (e.g., empty string or 1-2 characters) triggers the read.

The expression dl->length - so_ext_len is computed. Since both are size_t (unsigned), when dl->length < so_ext_len, this wraps to a very large value.

out_state.value: SIZE_MAX - 2 (huge value due to unsigned wrapping)

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause of the vulnerability: when dl->length is smaller than so_ext_len, unsigned arithmetic wraps, producing an out-of-bounds pointer passed to my_strcasecmp (which internally calls my_strcasecmp_8bit). This is exactly what the fix addresses by adding the `dl->length < so_ext_len` guard. While the CVE describes it as use-after-free/poison, the underlying issue is the same missing length check that causes invalid memory access in my_strcasecmp_8bit.

actual_issue_summary: The fix adds a bounds check `dl->length < so_ext_len` before computing `dl->str + dl->length - so_ext_len`. Without this check, when dl->length is smaller than so_ext_len, unsigned subtraction wraps to a huge value, causing my_strcasecmp (and internally my_strcasecmp_8bit) to read from an invalid/out-of-bounds memory location. The CVE describes this as use-after-free/poison in my_strcasecmp_8bit.

When extracted, the symbolic link is followed outside of the target dir allowing writing arbitrary files on the target host. In some cases, this can allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code. The vulnerable code is in the ZipFile::uncompressEntry function in juce_ZipFile.cpp and is executed when the archive is extracted upon calling uncompressTo() on a ZipFile object.

undesired_operation.impact: Arbitrary file write outside the intended extraction directory, potentially leading to remote code execution by overwriting critical files (e.g., cron jobs, SSH keys, application binaries).

The fix adds `targetFile.isAChildOf(targetDirectory)` check and symlink following protection. While the CVE specifically highlights CWE-59 (symlink following), the path traversal (CWE-22) is also part of the same vulnerability, as the fix addresses both path traversal and symlink issues.

The function did not validate that the resolved target file path remained within the target directory, allowing: (1) path traversal via '../' sequences in entry filenames (CWE-22), and (2) symlink following where a malicious archive entry creates a symbolic link that subsequent entries follow to write outside the target directory (CWE-59). The fix adds an isAChildOf check to prevent path traversal and a hasSymbolicPart check to prevent symlink-based escapes.

The scrollbar code is only reachable when FEAT_GUI is enabled and gui.in_use is true, which is not the primary attack path. The core vulnerability involves general termcode matching/parsing reading past the typeahead buffer.

CVE-2022-2285 is about the missing NUL terminator on the typeahead buffer causing reads past valid data during general termcode matching, leading to an integer overflow in del_typebuf(). The horizontal scrollbar loop is a secondary GUI-only code path, not the primary attack vector.

actual_issue_summary: The typeahead buffer (tp) was not NUL-terminated after the valid data (at position tp[len]). This allowed various string scanning operations in check_termcode (like getdigits(), atoi(), STRNCMP with string functions) to read past the end of valid typeahead data. The erroneous return value from check_termcode could then cause an integer overflow in del_typebuf() in getchar.c. The fix adds `tp[len] = NUL;` to place a sentinel NUL byte after the valid typeahead data.

cve_desc: regcomp.c in Perl before 5.30.3 allows a buffer overflow via a crafted regular expression because of recursive S_study_chunk calls.

undesired_operation.description: Signed integer overflow in the data->pos_delta overflow guard itself. The expression `-counted * deltanext + (minnext + deltanext) * maxcount - minnext * mincount` is computed in signed arithmetic and can overflow before the comparison against `SSize_t_MAX - data->pos_delta`, making the overflow check ineffective and allowing a corrupted value to be added to data->pos_delta.
undesired_operation.impact: The overflow in the guard expression causes the check to evaluate as false when it should be true, allowing a wrapped/incorrect value to be assigned to data->pos_delta. This corrupts regex length metadata, potentially leading to undersized allocations and heap buffer overflows during regex execution.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2020-12723 is about recursive S_study_chunk calls via GOSUB that mutate the regexp program (intermediate language representation) while an outer call is still examining it. The fix adds a `mutate_ok` flag and `in_gosub` tracking on scan frames to prevent mutations (JOIN_EXACT, trie optimization, CURLYX=>CURLYN/CURLYM optimizations, etc.) when processing within a GOSUB context. The finding describes a hypothetical signed integer overflow in a pos_delta guard expression, which is unrelated to the actual buffer overflow caused by mutating regexp ops during nested GOSUB study_chunk processing.

actual_issue_summary: When S_study_chunk processes a GOSUB node, it recurses (via enframing) into the referenced subpattern. During this inner processing, various optimization passes (JOIN_EXACT, trie construction, CURLYX=>CURLYN/CURLYM conversion, EXACTFU_S_EDGE=>EXACTFU, EXACT=>ANYOFM) could mutate the regexp program nodes. If an outer call was still iterating over those same nodes, the mutations would corrupt the outer call's view of the program, causing a buffer overflow. The fix introduces a `was_mutate_ok`/`mutate_ok` parameter and an `in_gosub` flag on scan frames to prevent any mutations to the regexp program when processing within a GOSUB context.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions several TensorFlow operations are missing validation for the shapes of the tensor arguments involved in the call. Depending on the API, this can result in undefined behavior and segfault or `CHECK`-fail related crashes but in some scenarios writes and reads from heap populated arrays are also possible. We have discovered these issues internally via tooling while working on improving/testing GPU op determinism. As such, we don't have reproducers and there will be multiple fixes for these issues. These fixes will be included in TensorFlow 2.7.0. We will also cherrypick these commits on TensorFlow 2.6.1, TensorFlow 2.5.2, and TensorFlow 2.4.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

'batch_size' is derived from input's batch dimensions, but the loop iterates batch_size times over transposed_rhs_reshaped (derived from rhs). If input's batch dimensions product is larger than rhs's, out-of-bounds memory access occurs.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. The code assumes `index` is a scalar but there is no validation for this before accessing its value. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

cve_desc: NULL Pointer Dereference in Homebrew mruby prior to 3.2.

undesired_operation.description: When val=0, codegen calls do not push values onto the compile-time stack, but pop_n(len*2) is still executed unconditionally when a NODE_KW_REST_ARGS node is encountered after normal key-value pairs. This pops stack entries that were never pushed, causing the compile-time stack pointer (cursp()) to underflow, leading to out-of-bounds memory access in subsequent genop instructions that reference the corrupted stack position.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack pointer underflow corrupts codegen state; subsequent genop calls use a negative or invalid stack index, leading to out-of-bounds writes into the bytecode or stack map, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution or a crash.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause of CVE-2022-0481: when val=0, the pop_n/pop operations execute without corresponding pushes, causing stack pointer corruption. The fix adds 'val &&' checks to both conditionals, exactly matching what the finding describes. While the CVE is classified as CWE-476 (NULL Pointer Dereference) — likely the crash symptom from the corrupted stack pointer — the finding identifies the same underlying bug (missing val check) even if it characterizes the consequence differently (out-of-bounds write vs NULL deref). The bug and the fix match precisely.

actual_issue_summary: In gen_hash(), when val=0 (meaning the hash value is not needed), pop_n() and pop() were still called in the NODE_KW_REST_ARGS branch without corresponding pushes, causing compile-time stack pointer underflow. This led to a NULL pointer dereference or crash. The fix adds 'val &&' guards to both conditional blocks to skip stack manipulation when val is 0.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions if the arguments to `tf.raw_ops.RaggedGather` don't determine a valid ragged tensor code can trigger a read from outside of bounds of heap allocated buffers. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/8d72537c6abf5a44103b57b9c2e22c14f5f49698/tensorflow/core/kernels/ragged_gather_op.cc#L70) directly reads the first dimension of a tensor shape before checking that said tensor has rank of at least 1 (i.e., it is not a scalar). Furthermore, the implementation does not check that the list given by `params_nested_splits` is not an empty list of tensors. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit a2b743f6017d7b97af1fe49087ae15f0ac634373. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: When params_nested_splits_in[0] is an empty tensor (dim_size(0) == 0), subtracting 1 causes an integer underflow. If SPLITS_TYPE is unsigned, num_params wraps to a very large value (e.g., 2^64-1 for uint64), causing ValidateIndices to accept any index. This leads to out-of-bounds memory access in MakeSplits when those unchecked indices are used to index into the splits tensors.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash, or potential information disclosure through out-of-bounds reads when attacker-controlled indices are used against empty or small splits tensors

judgments.reasoning: The finding identifies the same vulnerable code line and the same general class of issue as the CVE. The CVE describes that dim_size(0) is read before verifying the tensor has rank >= 1 (not a scalar), leading to heap OOB reads. The finding focuses on a related scenario where dim_size(0) returns 0, causing underflow on subtraction. Both scenarios stem from the same root cause: missing validation before `params_nested_splits_in[0].dim_size(0) - 1`. The fix adds checks for both non-empty list and non-scalar tensor, which addresses both the CVE's described issue and the finding's described issue.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerable code in RaggedGather has two missing checks: (1) it doesn't verify params_nested_splits is non-empty before accessing element [0], and (2) it doesn't verify params_nested_splits[0] has rank >= 1 before calling dim_size(0). Both lead to heap OOB reads. The fix adds OP_REQUIRES checks for params_nested_splits_in.size() > 0 and params_nested_splits_in[0].dims() > 0, replacing a debug-only DCHECK_GT with a proper runtime check.

In add_slot_store (and remove_slot_store), memcpy copies user data without null-terminating the drc_name buffer. strchr then searches for '
' past the copied data into uninitialized stack memory. If '

cve_desc: A flaw was found in libvirt in the virConnectListAllNodeDevices API in versions before 7.0.0. It only affects hosts with a PCI device and driver that supports mediated devices (e.g., GRID driver). This flaw could be used by an unprivileged client with a read-only connection to crash the libvirt daemon by executing the 'nodedev-list' virsh command. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to system availability.

undesired_operation.description: Due to C operator precedence, `*mdev_types[i]` is parsed as `*(mdev_types[i])` instead of the intended `(*mdev_types)[i]`. When i > 0, `mdev_types[i]` reads memory at offsets beyond the `mdev_types` pointer parameter itself, leading to out-of-bounds memory access and crash.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from stack or nearby memory. The value read is then dereferenced and passed to a free-like function, potentially leading to a crash (denial of service) or use of attacker-influenced pointer values for memory corruption.

>= 1 (at least one mdev type exists from prior population)

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: *nmdev_types >= 1 and i == 0, so the loop body executes

step_by_step_execution.description: First iteration: *mdev_types[0] is evaluated. Since mdev_types[0] == mdev_types itself (pointer arithmetic on the parameter), this dereferences *mdev_types which is the actual array pointer, then accesses element [0] — this happens to work correctly for i=0.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: If *nmdev_types >= 2, second iteration begins with i=1

step_by_step_execution.description: Second iteration: *mdev_types[1] is evaluated as *(mdev_types[1]). mdev_types[1] advances the pointer past the parameter itself into adjacent stack/memory, reading an arbitrary value. That value is then dereferenced and passed to virMediatedDeviceTypeFree, causing a segfault.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the operator precedence bug (`*mdev_types[i]` vs `(*mdev_types)[i]`) that causes out-of-bounds memory access and crash. This is exactly the vulnerability fixed by the commit and described in CVE-2021-3559. The CWEs differ (CWE-843/CWE-125 vs CWE-119) but CWE-125 is a child of CWE-119, and the underlying issue described is the same.

actual_issue_summary: Incorrect C operator precedence in virNodeDeviceGetMdevTypesCaps: `*mdev_types[i]` was parsed as `*(mdev_types[i])` instead of the intended `(*mdev_types)[i]`, causing out-of-bounds memory access when iterating over mdev types during a refresh. This could be triggered by an unprivileged client running 'nodedev-list', crashing the libvirt daemon.

cve_desc: In FreeRDP less than or equal to 2.0.0, an Integer Overflow to Buffer Overflow exists. When using /video redirection, a manipulated server can instruct the client to allocate a buffer with a smaller size than requested due to an integer overflow in size calculation. With later messages, the server can manipulate the client to write data out of bound to the previously allocated buffer. This has been patched in 2.1.0.

Subsequent writes into surfaceData during video decoding will overflow this undersized buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow during video frame decoding — attacker-controlled width/height from the presentation descriptor can cause a tiny allocation followed by large writes, leading to remote code execution or crash.

width * height * 4 is computed as a 32-bit multiplication (UINT32 * UINT32 * int), which overflows for large width/height values (e.g., width=65536, height=65536 gives 65536*65536*4 = 2^34 which truncates to 0 in 32-bit)

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies the CVE-2020-11038 vulnerability: an integer overflow in the 'width * height * 4' computation in PresentationContext_new leads to an undersized buffer allocation via BufferPool_Take, which is later written out of bounds during video decoding. The fix computes the size using 4ULL to avoid overflow and adds a bounds check against INT32_MAX.

actual_issue_summary: Integer overflow in PresentationContext_new where 'width * height * 4' is computed using 32-bit arithmetic for the BufferPool_Take size argument. A malicious server can send large width/height values that cause the multiplication to overflow, allocating a smaller buffer than needed. Subsequent video frame writes exceed the buffer, causing heap corruption. The fix uses 4ULL for 64-bit arithmetic and adds a check that the result does not exceed INT32_MAX.

cve_desc: Spice, versions 0.5.2 through 0.14.1, are vulnerable to an out-of-bounds read due to an off-by-one error in memslot_get_virt. This may lead to a denial of service, or, in the worst case, code-execution by unauthenticated attackers.

cve_desc: Directory traversal vulnerability in the client in HexChat 2.11.0 allows remote IRC servers to read or modify arbitrary files via a .. (dot dot) in the server name.

undesired_operation.description: Repeated strcat calls into a fixed 256-byte stack buffer overflow it when the server sends duplicate capability names in the extensions_str.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack-based buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution. A malicious IRC server controls extensions_str and can craft a CAP LS response with duplicate capability names to overflow the buffer.

judgments.reasoning: CVE-2016-2087 is a directory traversal vulnerability (CWE-22) allowing remote IRC servers to read or modify arbitrary files via '..' in the server name. The finding describes a stack buffer overflow in the CAP LS handler due to unbounded strcat. While the commit does change strcat to g_strlcat (fixing a potential overflow), this is not the same vulnerability as CVE-2016-2087. The commit appears to be a code cleanup that may have been misattributed to this CVE; the actual CVE is about path traversal, not buffer overflow.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2016-2087 is a directory traversal vulnerability where a malicious IRC server can use '..' sequences in the server name to read or modify arbitrary files on the HexChat client's filesystem. The provided commit cleans up the CAP LS handler by replacing unsafe strcat with bounded g_strlcat and using a whitelist array for supported capabilities, but this appears to be a tangential hardening change rather than the primary directory traversal fix.

cve_desc: Rizin is a UNIX-like reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset. Versions 0.4.0 and prior are vulnerable to an out-of-bounds write when getting data from dyld cache files. A user opening a malicious dyld cache file could be affected by this vulnerability, allowing an attacker to execute code on the user's machine. Commit number 556ca2f9eef01ec0f4a76d1fbacfcf3a87a44810 contains a patch.

undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow allowing arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker crafting a malicious dyld shared cache file can control the overflow data.

The overflow causes a too-small heap allocation, but the inner loops still iterate based on the original large n_slide_infos values, resulting in out-of-bounds heap writes. The fix changes total_slide_infos to size_t and adds an explicit overflow check.

cve_desc: The kernel in Amazon Web Services FreeRTOS before 10.4.3 has an integer overflow in queue.c for queue creation.

undesired_operation.description: Integer overflow in uxQueueLength * uxItemSize produces a small xQueueSizeInBytes, leading to an undersized heap allocation. The overflow check is in configASSERT which is typically compiled out in release/production builds. When the queue is subsequently used with the original uxQueueLength and uxItemSize, writes will exceed the allocated buffer.
code_snippets: pxNewQueue = ( Queue_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( Queue_t ) + xQueueSizeInBytes );
prvInitialiseNewQueue( uxQueueLength, uxItemSize, pucQueueStorage, ucQueueType, pxNewQueue );
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow — when items are enqueued using the original uxQueueLength and uxItemSize, writes go past the undersized allocation, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution or system corruption.

step_by_step_execution.description: xQueueSizeInBytes is computed as uxQueueLength * uxItemSize, which overflows
pvPortMalloc allocates sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes bytes, which is undersized

judgments.reasoning: The finding focuses on the multiplication overflow (uxQueueLength * uxItemSize), which already had a configASSERT check in the vulnerable code. The actual CVE (CVE-2021-31571) and its fix address a missing check for the ADDITION overflow in `sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes`. The fix adds `configASSERT((sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes) > xQueueSizeInBytes)` to catch when the addition of sizeof(Queue_t) to xQueueSizeInBytes wraps around. The finding identifies the wrong specific integer overflow.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerability is a missing integer overflow check on the addition `sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes` in xQueueGenericCreate(). Even when the multiplication `uxQueueLength * uxItemSize` doesn't overflow, adding sizeof(Queue_t) to the result could wrap around, causing pvPortMalloc to allocate a too-small buffer. The fix adds a configASSERT to detect this addition overflow.

cve_desc: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command in bookmarking function of Newsbeuter versions 0.7 through 2.9 allows remote attackers to perform user-assisted code execution by crafting an RSS item that includes shell code in its title and/or URL.

undesired_operation.description: The title, description, and feed_title parameters are interpolated into a shell command string without proper shell escaping. stfl::quote is designed for STFL UI library quoting, not shell metacharacter escaping. An attacker controlling RSS feed metadata can inject arbitrary OS commands.

std::string cmdline = strprintf::fmt("%s '%s' %s %s %s",
		                                       bookmark_cmd,
		                                       utils::replace_all(url,"'", "%27"),
		                                       quote_empty(stfl::quote(title)),
		                                       quote_empty(stfl::quote(description)),
		                                       quote_empty(stfl::quote(feed_title)));
undesired_operation.impact: Arbitrary command execution — an attacker who controls an RSS feed can set a malicious title/description/feed_title containing shell metacharacters to execute arbitrary commands when a user bookmarks an article.

This matches the CVE description exactly.

actual_issue_summary: The bookmark function in Newsbeuter did not properly shell-escape the title, description, and feed_title parameters when constructing a shell command. These were passed through stfl::quote (a UI library quoting function) instead of proper shell escaping, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary shell commands via crafted RSS feed metadata. The fix replaces stfl::quote/quote_empty with single-quote wrapping and replace_all to escape embedded single quotes.

Could result in arbitrary code execution or denial of service.

The fix rejects non-positive width/height values and adds an overflow check on the size computation.

cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read in the BGP daemon of FRRouting FRR before 8.4 may lead to a segmentation fault and denial of service. This occurs in bgp_capability_msg_parse in bgpd/bgp_packet.c.

The fix adds a check ensuring hdr->length >= sizeof(struct capability_mp_data) before proceeding.

cve_desc: An issue was discovered in net/tipc/crypto.c in the Linux kernel before 5.14.16. The Transparent Inter-Process Communication (TIPC) functionality allows remote attackers to exploit insufficient validation of user-supplied sizes for the MSG_CRYPTO message type.

The missing 'return -1' in the error-handling if-block causes buffer and path to be freed twice. This matches CVE-2012-4559 which describes multiple double-free vulnerabilities in libssh, including in sftp.c.
