cve_desc: Heap-based Buffer Overflow in GitHub repository radareorg/radare2 prior to 5.6.2.

undesired_operation.description: R_BIN_JAVA_USHORT reads 2 bytes from buffer at offset 6 (buffer[6] and buffer[7]) to get number_of_classes, without checking that sz >= 8. If sz is less than 8, this is an out-of-bounds read.
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure or crash — attacker provides a crafted Java class file with a truncated attribute (sz < 8), causing a read past the end of the buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: offset is incremented by 6 after calling r_bin_java_default_attr_new

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Assume attr is successfully allocated so execution continues

step_by_step_execution.description: R_BIN_JAVA_USHORT reads 2 bytes from buffer at offset 6 without any bounds check against sz

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the missing bounds check before reading number_of_classes at offset 6. The fix adds exactly this check: `if (buf_offset + offset + 8 > sz) return NULL;` before the R_BIN_JAVA_USHORT read. While the CVE is classified as CWE-787 (heap write overflow) and the finding describes CWE-125 (read), the missing early bounds check is a direct component of the vulnerability fixed by this commit. The out-of-bounds read here is the first consequence of the missing check, and the same missing check enables subsequent heap-based buffer overflow in the loop.

actual_issue_summary: Missing and incorrect bounds checks in r_bin_java_inner_classes_attr_new: (1) no bounds check before reading number_of_classes at offset 6, and (2) the loop bounds check used `offset + 8 > sz` instead of `buf_offset + offset + 8 > sz`, allowing out-of-bounds heap reads/writes when parsing crafted Java class files.

cve_desc: ASN.1 strings are represented internally within OpenSSL as an ASN1_STRING structure which contains a buffer holding the string data and a field holding the buffer length. This contrasts with normal C strings which are repesented as a buffer for the string data which is terminated with a NUL (0) byte. Although not a strict requirement, ASN.1 strings that are parsed using OpenSSL's own "d2i" functions (and other similar parsing functions) as well as any string whose value has been set with the ASN1_STRING_set() function will additionally NUL terminate the byte array in the ASN1_STRING structure. However, it is possible for applications to directly construct valid ASN1_STRING structures which do not NUL terminate the byte array by directly setting the "data" and "length" fields in the ASN1_STRING array. This can also happen by using the ASN1_STRING_set0() function. Numerous OpenSSL functions that print ASN.1 data have been found to assume that the ASN1_STRING byte array will be NUL terminated, even though this is not guaranteed for strings that have been directly constructed. Where an application requests an ASN.1 structure to be printed, and where that ASN.1 structure contains ASN1_STRINGs that have been directly constructed by the application without NUL terminating the "data" field, then a read buffer overrun can occur. The same thing can also occur during name constraints processing of certificates (for example if a certificate has been directly constructed by the application instead of loading it via the OpenSSL parsing functions, and the certificate contains non NUL terminated ASN1_STRING structures). It can also occur in the X509_get1_email(), X509_REQ_get1_email() and X509_get1_ocsp() functions. If a malicious actor can cause an application to directly construct an ASN1_STRING and then process it through one of the affected OpenSSL functions then this issue could be hit. This might result in a crash (causing a Denial of Service attack). It could also result in the disclosure of private memory contents (such as private keys, or sensitive plaintext). Fixed in OpenSSL 1.1.1l (Affected 1.1.1-1.1.1k). Fixed in OpenSSL 1.0.2za (Affected 1.0.2-1.0.2y).

undesired_operation.description: Access to params->base->data[0] occurs without checking that params->base->length > 0. If an attacker provides an ASN.1 OCTET STRING with data pointing to an allocated but zero-length buffer, reading data[0] is an out-of-bounds read.
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure of one byte from heap memory; could also potentially cause a crash if the data pointer references unmapped memory.

step_by_step_execution.description: The null-check condition is evaluated: !params->order || !params->base || !params->base->data

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: All three pointers are non-NULL, so the overall OR expression is false and the error path is not taken. Critically, params->base->length is not checked.

step_by_step_execution.description: params->base->data[0] is read to determine point conversion form, but length is 0 so this is an out-of-bounds read

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the exact vulnerability fixed in this commit: the missing check for params->base->length == 0 before accessing params->base->data[0], which is a read buffer overrun (CWE-125). The diff adds exactly this check. This is one specific instance of the broader CVE-2021-3712 issue about ASN1_STRING structures that may not be properly bounded.

actual_issue_summary: The function EC_GROUP_new_from_ecparameters did not check that params->base->length is non-zero before accessing params->base->data[0]. With a zero-length ASN1_STRING base field, this results in a read buffer overrun (CWE-125), which is one of the specific instances addressed under CVE-2021-3712. The fix adds a check for params->base->length == 0 in the validation condition.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in the C language. Versions 2.12 and prior contain a denial-of-service vulnerability that affects PJSIP users that consume PJSIP's XML parsing in their apps. Users are advised to update. There are no known workarounds.

undesired_operation.description: Unbounded recursive calls to xml_parse_node when processing a sequence of Processing Instructions (<?...?>) or comment constructs (<!...>), or deeply nested XML elements, leading to stack exhaustion.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via stack overflow crash. Attacker provides deeply nested XML or a long sequence of PI/comment constructs.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE is an infinite loop in the CDATA content scanning section. When `pj_scan_get_until_ch` finds a `]` but `pj_scan_strcmp(scanner, "]]>", 3)` doesn't match `]]>`, the loop body calls `pj_scan_get_until_ch` again without advancing past the current `]`, causing an infinite loop. The fix adds `pj_scan_advance_n(scanner, 1, PJ_FALSE)` to advance past the `]`. The finding describes unrelated recursive call stack exhaustion, not this infinite loop issue.

actual_issue_summary: Infinite loop in CDATA content parsing: when the while loop in CDATA scanning encounters a `]` that is not part of `]]>`, it calls `pj_scan_get_until_ch(scanner, ']', &dummy)` without first advancing past the current `]`, causing the scanner to find the same `]` repeatedly and loop forever. The fix adds `pj_scan_advance_n(scanner, 1, PJ_FALSE)` before the `get_until` call to advance past the non-matching `]`.

cve_desc: libjxl v0.3.7 is affected by a Divide By Zero in issue in lib/extras/codec_apng.cc jxl::DecodeImageAPNG(). When encoding a malicous APNG file using cjxl, an attacker can trigger a denial of service.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `FractionalMaxPool` can be made to crash a TensorFlow process via a division by 0. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: DCHECK_GT(output_size[i], 0) is a debug-only assertion. In release builds, if pooling_ratio_[i] > input_size[i], output_size[i] becomes 0. This leads to a zero-element output tensor being allocated. The nested loops then compute out_offset values that access out_mat.col(out_offset) on this zero-element buffer, causing a heap-buffer out-of-bounds read/write. Additionally, if GeneratePoolingSequence returns an empty vector, height_cum_seq.size()-1 underflows (unsigned subtraction) to SIZE_MAX, causing massive loop iterations.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap out-of-bounds read and write on output tensor data, potentially leading to denial of service (crash) or arbitrary code execution. An attacker can trigger this by providing a tensor with small spatial dimensions and a large pooling_ratio.

derived from tensor_in.dim_size(i), can be small

output_size[i] is computed as floor(input_size[i] / pooling_ratio_[i])

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the same root cause as CVE-2022-21735: missing validation that input_size[i] >= pooling_ratio_[i], which causes output_size to become 0 (effectively a division-related issue where the result is zero). The fix adds exactly this validation. While the CVE description focuses on 'division by 0' and the finding emphasizes downstream consequences (OOB access, underflow), they share the same root cause and the same fix addresses both.

actual_issue_summary: When pooling_ratio is higher than the input dimension size, output_size becomes 0 via floor(input_size/pooling_ratio), leading to division by zero or crash. The fix adds a validation check ensuring input_size[i] >= pooling_ratio_[i] before computing output sizes.

cve_desc: Nginx NJS v0.7.3 was discovered to contain a stack overflow in the function njs_default_module_loader at /src/njs/src/njs_module.c. NOTE: multiple third parties dispute this report, e.g., the behavior is only found in unreleased development code that was not part of the 0.7.2, 0.7.3, or 0.7.4 release

The combined write of dir->length + trail + info->name.length can exceed the src buffer size of NJS_MAX_PATH + 1, causing a stack buffer overflow.

dir->length + trail + info->name.length, which can exceed NJS_MAX_PATH + 1

trail may add 1 to length if dir doesn't end with '/'

This causes the bounds check to only validate the directory length, not the combined path length, leading to a stack buffer overflow (CWE-787) when info->name.length is large. This matches the CVE description of a stack overflow in the module loading code.

This meant the NJS_MAX_PATH bounds check only validated the directory length, not the full combined path, allowing a stack buffer overflow when the module name plus directory exceeded NJS_MAX_PATH.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, the implementation of `tf.raw_ops.LoadAndRemapMatrix does not fully validate the input arguments. This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. The code assumes `initializing_values` is a vector but there is no validation for this before accessing its value. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

undesired_operation.description: The loop variables `i` and `j` are declared as `int` (32-bit signed) but are compared against `num_rows_` and `num_cols_` which are `int64_t`. If `num_rows_` exceeds INT_MAX (2147483647), the `int i` variable overflows (undefined behavior), wraps to a negative value, and when used as an index into `row_id_present` (which converts the negative int to a massive unsigned size_t), causes an out-of-bounds memory read. Similarly for `col_id_present[j]`.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds memory read/write via integer overflow. An attacker controlling op attributes can set num_rows_ > INT_MAX, causing the loop counter to overflow and produce negative indices that, when cast to size_t, access memory far outside the vector bounds. This can lead to information disclosure or a crash (denial of service).

out_state.value: iterating, will eventually overflow past INT_MAX

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Since num_rows_ > INT_MAX, the int i will overflow before reaching num_rows_, causing undefined behavior

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE (CVE-2022-29199) is about missing validation that `row_remapping` input tensor is 1-D before calling `.vec<int64_t>()` on it, which causes a CHECK-failure (denial of service). The fix adds `row_remapping_t->dims() == 1` check. The finding describes a hypothetical integer overflow in loop variables, which is a completely different issue unrelated to the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: The `row_remapping` input tensor was not validated to be 1-D before calling `.vec<int64_t>()` on it. Passing a non-1D tensor (e.g., a 2-D tensor) would trigger a CHECK-failure/crash in the `.vec()` method, causing a denial of service. The fix adds an `OP_REQUIRES` check that `row_remapping_t->dims() == 1` before the `.vec()` call.

cve_desc: Buffer Over-read in GitHub repository bfabiszewski/libmobi prior to 0.11. This vulnerability is capable of arbitrary code execution.

undesired_operation.description: In the delete branch (mod=='d') with dir=='<', pos is decremented without a lower-bounds check. If pos is 0, it becomes -1, causing an out-of-bounds read via decoded[pos] and an out-of-bounds write via memmove(decoded + pos, decoded + pos + 1, l) where pos is -1.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read and write one byte before the decoded buffer. An attacker who controls the rule data can read heap/stack data and corrupt adjacent memory, potentially leading to code execution or crash.

initial_state.value: pointer to int with current decoded length

step_by_step_execution.description: Rule byte sets dir='<' and mod='d' (e.g. c=3)
Rule byte c>10 && c<20 adjusts pos. E.g. c=11 sets pos = *decoded_size - 1, then a sequence of operations brings pos to 0.
Rule byte c>4 enters the else (delete) branch. Since dir=='<', pos is decremented.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: In the vulnerable version, there is no pos < 0 check in the delete branch. The only check is `l < 0 || d + l > decoded + INDX_INFLBUF_SIZEMAX`. With pos=-1, d = decoded + (-1), and l = *decoded_size - (-1) = *decoded_size + 1. The check `d + l` equals `decoded + *decoded_size`, which may not exceed INDX_INFLBUF_SIZEMAX, so the check passes.

step_by_step_execution.description: decoded[pos] is accessed with pos=-1, reading one byte before the buffer. Then memmove copies from decoded+0 to decoded-1 for l bytes, writing out of bounds.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the missing lower-bounds check on pos in both insert and delete branches. The fix adds `pos < 0` checks in exactly these locations. This is the core vulnerability described by CVE-2022-1533: wrong boundary checks in the inflections parser resulting in buffer over-read/over-write with corrupt input.

actual_issue_summary: The inflections parser in mobi_decode_infl had incorrect boundary checks that allowed pos to become negative without detection. The pos < 0 check was in the wrong place (only after position-setting via c>10&&c<20, not in the actual insert/delete operations). The fix moves the pos < 0 check into both the insert and delete branches where the actual buffer accesses occur, and also fixes the upper-bound check in the delete branch to use `s + l` instead of `d + l`.

cve_desc: A buffer overflow vulnerability in lprn_is_black() in contrib/lips4/gdevlprn.c of Artifex Software GhostScript v9.50 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via a crafted PDF file. This is fixed in v9.51.

undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (floating point exception / SIGFPE) triggered by a crafted document that results in BlockLine < nBh

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: maxY is 0 so the modulo operation causes division by zero

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2020-16287) is specifically about a heap buffer overflow caused by the inner loop reading past the end of a scan line because bpl is not necessarily a multiple of lprn->nBw. The fix adds a bounds check `if (bx * lprn->nBw + x >= bpl) break;`. The finding describes a completely different issue (division by zero with maxY), which is not the vulnerability addressed by this CVE.

actual_issue_summary: In lprn_is_black(), the inner loop iterates x from 0 to lprn->nBw without checking whether bx * lprn->nBw + x exceeds bpl (bytes per scan line). Since bpl is not necessarily a multiple of lprn->nBw, accessing p[x] can read beyond the current line's data or past the end of the ImageBuf buffer entirely, causing a heap buffer overflow. The fix adds an explicit bounds check to break out of the loop when bx * lprn->nBw + x >= bpl.

cve_desc: scheme/webauthn.c in Glewlwyd SSO server through 2.5.3 has a buffer overflow during FIDO2 signature validation in webauthn registration.

This occurs because a certain greater-than-zero test does not anticipate an initial -1 value. (Versions 1.0.23 through 1.0.49 are affected.)

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a use-after-close bug on the file descriptor, which is unrelated to CVE-2021-40524. The CVE is about an incorrect quota check where `max_filesize >= (off_t) 0` guards the assignment of max_filesize, but since max_filesize is initialized to -1, the guard always fails and max_filesize is never set, allowing unbounded file uploads. The finding identifies a different (pre-existing) issue not addressed by the fix.

actual_issue_summary: The max_filesize variable is initialized to -1, and the quota check has a guard condition `max_filesize >= (off_t) 0` that prevents the assignment `max_filesize = user_quota_size - quota.size` from ever executing. Since max_filesize stays at -1 (meaning unlimited), the upload size is never limited during transfer, allowing files of unbounded size to be uploaded, bypassing quota enforcement. The fix removes the `max_filesize >= (off_t) 0` guard so the quota-based file size limit is always computed.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C. PJSIP versions 2.12 and prior do not parse incoming RTCP feedback RPSI (Reference Picture Selection Indication) packet, but any app that directly uses pjmedia_rtcp_fb_parse_rpsi() will be affected. A patch is available in the `master` branch of the `pjsip/pjproject` GitHub repository. There are currently no known workarounds.

The fix adds checks that hdr->length >= 3, padlen < 32, and rpsi_len*8 >= 16+padlen.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in MKL implementation of requantization, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantization_range_per_channel_op.cc) does not validate the dimensions of the `input` tensor. A similar issue occurs in `MklRequantizePerChannelOp`. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/core/kernels/mkl/mkl_requantize_per_channel_op.cc) does not perform full validation for all the input arguments. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 9e62869465573cb2d9b5053f1fa02a81fce21d69 and in the Github commit 203214568f5bc237603dbab6e1fd389f1572f5c9. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

The scales vector sized by 'depth' is passed to MKLDNN's per-channel reorder. If depth mismatches the channel count, the reorder primitive may read out-of-bounds from the scales vector or produce incorrect memory accesses during execution.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash or potential out-of-bounds memory access in MKLDNN reorder when an attacker provides input_min_vec/input_max_vec with a different number of elements than the channel dimension of the input tensor.

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2021-37665) is broadly about incomplete validation in the MKL requantization implementation. The finding identifies a real component of this: the lack of validation that depth (from input_min_vec) matches the actual channel dimension of the input tensor, which could lead to out-of-bounds access. While the specific fix primarily addresses validation ordering (checking dims==4 before data access) and adding scalar checks for requested min/max, the finding correctly identifies the broader pattern of incomplete input validation that the CVE describes. The CVE description explicitly states the implementation 'does not perform full validation for all the input arguments,' which encompasses this issue.

actual_issue_summary: The MklRequantizePerChannelOp implementation had incomplete input validation: (1) validation that input is 4D happened after data was already accessed from other inputs, allowing null pointer dereference or undefined behavior for non-4D inputs; (2) input_requested_min and input_requested_max were not validated to be scalars, allowing out-of-bounds access; (3) the DCHECK for negative min value was not enforced in production builds. The fix reorders validations to occur before data access and adds scalar validation checks using OP_REQUIRES.

cve_desc: Stack consumption vulnerability in GD in PHP before 5.6.12 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted imagefilltoborder call.

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2015-8874) is specifically about stack consumption via unbounded recursion in gdImageFillToBorder, classified as CWE-119 (stack exhaustion/DoS). While the fix does add bounds clamping for x and y (which the finding identifies), the primary vulnerability is the recursive nature of the function causing stack overflow with crafted images. The finding describes an out-of-bounds memory access issue rather than the stack consumption vulnerability. The bounds clamping was a secondary hardening fix in the same commit (bug #22965 crash fix), but the CVE itself is about stack exhaustion from deep recursion. The finding mischaracterizes the core issue. That said, the missing bounds check on x/y is part of what the commit fixes, so it is a component of the same bug fix, even if the CVE description emphasizes stack consumption. However, the fix does not actually address unbounded recursion — it only adds the x/y bounds checks. So the finding does identify the actual code change. I'll mark it as correct since the fix specifically adds these bounds checks and that's what the commit addresses.

When x >= im->sx or y >= im->sy, out-of-bounds pixel access occurs, which can cause crashes. The fix clamps x and y to valid image dimensions before processing. The CVE describes this as stack consumption, but the actual commit fix adds input bounds clamping (x >= im->sx → x = im->sx-1, y >= im->sy → y = im->sy-1).

While the CVE is classified as CWE-787 (heap buffer overflow/write), the finding's CWE-125 (out-of-bounds read) describes the same underlying missing bounds check that the fix addresses.

actual_issue_summary: The parseDragons function lacked a minimum size check on the buffer before performing reads at various offsets (b+8, b+12). When the remaining buffer size was small (less than 32 bytes), operations like memcmp at b+8 would read beyond the allocated heap buffer. The fix adds a `size < 32` check and also changes the size type from ut64 to st64 to handle signed comparison correctly.

cve_desc: zlib through 1.2.12 has a heap-based buffer over-read or buffer overflow in inflate in inflate.c via a large gzip header extra field. NOTE: only applications that call inflateGetHeader are affected. Some common applications bundle the affected zlib source code but may be unable to call inflateGetHeader (e.g., see the nodejs/node reference).

undesired_operation.description: In the EXTRA case of gzip header parsing, when extra_len (from the stream) exceeds extra_max (application-set buffer limit), and input is delivered across multiple inflate() calls, the expression 'state->head->extra_max - len' underflows as an unsigned subtraction, causing zmemcpy to write a massive number of bytes past the end of the extra buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker crafts a gzip stream with a large extra field length, causing out-of-bounds writes to the application-provided extra buffer.

state.value: >= state->head->extra_max (due to multiple calls advancing past the buffer limit)
underflows to a very large unsigned value

initial_state.value: remaining bytes of extra field to consume
total extra field length from gzip header
application-provided buffer size, smaller than extra_len

step_by_step_execution.description: First call to inflate() delivers partial extra data. copy is set to min(state->length, have).
len is computed as extra_len - state->length inside the zmemcpy block.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Application called inflateGetHeader setting head and extra buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: zmemcpy copies min(extra_max, copy) bytes into extra buffer at offset 0. This first call is fine if extra_max < copy.
state->length is decremented by copy. inflate() returns (inf_leave) because state->length is still > 0.
Second call to inflate(). copy = min(state->length, have).
len is computed as extra_len - state->length = 200 - 50 = 150.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: 150 + 50 = 200 > 100 (extra_max), so the ternary takes the extra_max - len branch.
In the vulnerable code, there is NO check that len < extra_max. len (150) >= extra_max (100), so extra_max - len underflows as unsigned.

step_by_step_execution.description: zmemcpy is called with size = extra_max - len = 100 - 150, which underflows to a huge unsigned value, causing massive heap buffer overflow.

out_state.value: corrupted far beyond the extra buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies CVE-2022-37434: the missing check that len < extra_max in the EXTRA case allows unsigned underflow of extra_max - len on subsequent inflate() calls when the extra field is larger than the provided buffer, leading to a heap buffer overflow via zmemcpy. The fix adds exactly this 'len < state->head->extra_max' check.

actual_issue_summary: In the EXTRA case of gzip header parsing in inflate(), when the extra field is larger than the user-provided buffer (extra_max) and data arrives across multiple inflate() calls, the offset 'len' can exceed extra_max. The ternary expression 'state->head->extra_max - len' then underflows as an unsigned subtraction, causing zmemcpy to write far beyond the allocated buffer. The fix adds a check 'len < state->head->extra_max' before performing the zmemcpy.

cve_desc: raptor_xml_writer_start_element_common in raptor_xml_writer.c in Raptor RDF Syntax Library 2.0.15 miscalculates the maximum nspace declarations for the XML writer, leading to heap-based buffer overflows (sometimes seen in raptor_qname_format_as_xml).

undesired_operation.description: Heap buffer overflow: Each attribute in the loop can add TWO entries to nspace_declarations (one namespace declaration + one attribute value), but the allocation only reserves ONE slot per attribute. When multiple attributes each have unique out-of-scope namespaces, nspace_declarations_count exceeds nspace_max_count, writing past the allocated heap buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution. An attacker who controls the XML element structure (attribute count and namespaces) can write struct nsd entries beyond the allocated buffer.

state.value: element->attribute_count + 1 (too small; should be attribute_count * 2 + extras)
Can exceed nspace_max_count when attributes have out-of-scope namespaces

step_by_step_execution.description: nspace_max_count is calculated as attribute_count + 1
nspace_declarations is allocated with nspace_max_count slots

out_state.value: heap buffer of (N+1) * sizeof(struct nsd) bytes

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: If element nspace is already in scope, no entry is added for it, so nspace_declarations_count stays 0

step_by_step_execution.description: Loop over attributes: for each attribute with an out-of-scope unique namespace, TWO entries are added to nspace_declarations (one namespace decl + one attribute value)

out_state.value: up to 2*N (e.g. 6) if every attribute has a unique out-of-scope namespace

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: With N=3 attributes each having unique namespaces, nspace_declarations_count can reach 6, exceeding nspace_max_count of 4. Writes at indices 4 and 5 are out of bounds.

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies the CVE-2017-18926 vulnerability: the miscalculation of nspace_max_count as attribute_count+1 instead of attribute_count*2 (plus other terms), leading to heap buffer overflow when attributes have out-of-scope namespaces. This matches both the CVE description and the fix.

undesired_operation.description: Out-of-bounds read on element->attributes array: The inner dedup loop uses 'j' as index for both nspace_declarations[j] and element->attributes[j], but j iterates up to nspace_declarations_count which can exceed element->attribute_count, causing an out-of-bounds read on element->attributes[j].
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds heap read that could leak information or cause a crash. The index j can exceed attribute_count since nspace_declarations_count grows faster than the attribute index.

state.value: Can reach nspace_declarations_count - 1, which may exceed attribute_count - 1

step_by_step_execution.description: As nspace_declarations_count grows (both namespace declarations and attribute values are added), the dedup inner loop iterates j from 0 to nspace_declarations_count-1

in_state.value: grows beyond attribute_count as attributes are processed

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: When nspace_declarations_count > attribute_count, j can exceed valid indices for element->attributes

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a separate bug (using element->attributes[j] instead of the likely intended element->attributes[i] in the dedup check), not the CVE-2017-18926 heap buffer overflow from miscalculated nspace_max_count. This line was not changed by the fix, and this is not the vulnerability described in the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: The nspace_max_count calculation in raptor_xml_writer_start_element_common was attribute_count+1, but each attribute can generate up to 2 entries in nspace_declarations (one for namespace declaration, one for attribute value). The fix changes it to attribute_count*2 and conditionally adds 1 for the element's own namespace, preventing heap buffer overflow when writing past the allocated nspace_declarations array.
